architecture.summary: L2M-AID fuses LLM semantic reasoning with multi-agent reinforcement learning (MAPPO) for industrial cyber-physical defense. An LLM acts as a semantic bridge, translating unstructured IIoT telemetry into contextual state representations that capture adversary intent. This semantically-enriched state feeds collaborative agents trained by MAPPO, which learn cooperative defense strategies optimized by a reward function balancing threat neutralization against physical process stability disruption penalties.

biblio.title: L2M-AID: Autonomous Cyber-Physical Defense by Fusing Semantic Reasoning of Large Language Models with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

claims.dataset: SWaT dataset and synthetic MITRE ATT&CK for ICS dataset
claims.statement: L2M-AID achieves a 97.2% detection rate while reducing false positives by over 80%

claims.statement: L2M-AID demonstrates superior performance in maintaining physical process stability
LLM as semantic bridge translates vast, unstructured telemetry into rich, contextual state representation, enabling agents to reason about adversary intent rather than merely matching patterns
